narration: Define a function named binarySearch that accepts an array, a low index, a high index, and a target value x as parameters.
Calculate the mid index using the formula (low + high) / 2, ensuring the result is an integer using Math.floor.
Check if the array element at the mid index equals the target value x. If true, return the mid index.
If the target value x is less than the array element at the mid index, recursively call binarySearch with the same low and mid - 1 as the new high.
If the target value x is greater than the array element at the mid index, recursively call binarySearch with mid + 1 as the new low and the same high.
Add a base case to the recursion: if low is greater than high, return a message indicating the element is not found.
Explain that binary search requires the input array to be sorted; otherwise, the algorithm will not work correctly.
Demonstrate the execution of binary search using a visualizer to trace the recursive steps.
Show how the algorithm narrows the search space by comparing the target value with the mid element and adjusting the low and high bounds.
Log the final index where the target value is found in the console.
Test the function with a target value that exists in the array to verify successful search.
Fix the stack overflow error by adding a check for low > high before the recursive call.
Run the corrected code to confirm the element not found message is displayed when the target is absent.
